trajectory.description: Observation: MetricGAN+ failed with cuDNN error (non-contiguous input) in iteration 5. Analysis: Relying on pretrained model is risky; we have a working ConvTasNet baseline (PESQ 2.365, val 2.75). Training longer with augmentation and better schedule should improve. Decision: Overwrite train.py with data augmentation (random gain, Gaussian noise), ReduceLROnPlateau, gradient clipping, early stopping, and resume from best checkpoint. Update predict.py to load our ConvTasNet. Train 15 epochs (~1.5 h) and submit eval to measure progress.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 9 eval PESQ dropped to 2.17 from baseline 2.36, despite val PESQ 2.61 at epoch 10. This indicates strong overfitting or generalization failure. Since we have a pre-downloaded MetricGAN+ speechbrain model, and previous attempt (iter 5) failed with cuDNN error (non-contiguous input), we can try to fix the inference and use that pretrained model to achieve a high PESQ (~3.0+) with no training. Decision: rewrite predict.py to load MetricGAN+ with correct contiguous input and relative lengths=1.0; test locally on one training file to verify it runs without cuDNN error; then submit eval immediately to potentially finish early with a strong score.
